initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<example_grid_1>

0 7 7

7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
</diagram>
</example_grid_1>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Input and colors already given.

% Determine grid dimensions (global)
maxRowGlobal(M) :- M = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(M) :- M = #min{R : input(R,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(N) :- N = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
minColGlobal(N) :- N = #min{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Determine min/max rows/cols of non-zero cells (for border selection)
maxRowNonZero(M) :- M = #max{R : input(R,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minRowNonZero(M) :- M = #min{R : input(R,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(N) :- N = #max{C : input(_,C,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(N) :- N = #min{C : input(_,C,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Define border cells on outermost non-zero boundary
border_on_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero; R = maxRowNonZero; C = minColNonZero; C = maxColNonZero).

% Count occurrences per color for these border cells
count_boundary_color(C) :- #count{R : border_on_boundary(R,C)}.

% Choose outer color: most frequent among border cells
outer_color(C) :-
    count_boundary_color(C),
    count_boundary_color(C2),
    count_boundary_color(C),
    count_boundary_color(C2),
    C != C2.

% Flood fill outside via non-outer_color cells
% define offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; outside_boundary(R,C).
outside(R,C) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != outer_color.

outside_boundary(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; input(R,C,V), V != outer_color.
outside_boundary(R,C) :-
    (R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)} ; input(R,C,V), V != outer_color).
outside_boundary(R,C) :-
    (R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)} ; input(R,C,V), V != outer_color).
outside_boundary(R,C) :-
    (C = 0 ; input(R,C,V), V != outer_color).
outside_boundary(R,C) :-
    (C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)} ; input(R,C,V), V != outer_color).

Note: the #max and #min functions may be used; but we can also directly use known global dimensions: define maxRowGlobal etc. Use those.

% Define outside predicate (simpler)
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal), input(R,C,V), V != outer_color.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != outer_color.

% Interior cells: non-outer_color not outside
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != outer_color, not outside(R,C).

% Determine bounds of interior region
interior_min_row(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
interior_max_row(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
interior_min_col(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
interior_max_col(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner predicate for interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = interior_min_row) or (R = interior_max_row) or (C = interior_min_col) or (C = interior_max_col).

% Output definition (choose exactly one color per interior cell)
1 { output(R,C,0) : interior(R,C), corner(R,C) } 1 :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,outer_color) : interior(R,C), not corner(R,C) } 1 :- interior(R,C).

<asp>
% Offsets for 4-connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Find min/max row/col indices for non‑zero cells (used to locate the shape)
minRowNZ(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNZ(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNZ(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNZ(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Border cells that lie on the outer boundary of the non‑zero region
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNZ) ; (R = maxRowNZ) ; (C = minColNZ) ; (C = maxColNZ).

% Count how many border cells have each colour
count_boundary_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% Choose the colour that occurs most often on the outer boundary
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_boundary_colour(C),
    count_boundary_colour(C2),
    count_boundary_colour(C),
    count_boundary_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_boundary_colour(C) > count_boundary_colour(C2).

% Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the outer colour
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% Cells that are inside the shape (cannot be reached from the boundary through non‑outer_colour cells)
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% Bounding box of the interior region
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% For interior cells we produce exactly one output colour:
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must have exactly one output fact.
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set: for each interior cell we need one output colour (either 0 or outer_colour)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

</asp>

%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),

outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------

maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

We'll define:

% Determine bounds of non-zero cells
minRow(NR) :- NR = #min{R : input(R, _, V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRow(NR) :- NR = #max{R : input(R, _, V), color(V), V != 0}.
minCol(NC) :- NC = #min{C : input(_, C, V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxCol(NC) :- NC = #max{C : input(_, C, V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Border cells: those where R = minRow or R = maxRow or C = minCol or C = maxCol
border(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, (R = minRow) ; (R = maxRow) ; (C = minCol) ; (C = maxCol).

% Count each colour on border (ignore zeros)
border_colour(C) :- border(R,C), color(C).

% Frame colour: colour with max count; tie broken arbitrarily (choose any with max)
frame_colour(F) :-
    border_colour(F),
    #count{R : border_colour(R)},
    F != R2,
    #count{R2 : border_colour(R2)},
    #count{R : border_colour(R)} >= #count{R2 : border_colour(R2)},
    F = R2 -> false.  % This is tricky.

```
% Determine the bounding rectangle of all non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.
minRowNZ(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
maxRowNZ(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C)}.
maxColNZ(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : nonzero(_,C)}.

% Border cells of the non-zero rectangle (including zeros? but we will count only non-zero for colour)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, (R = minRowNZ) ; (R = maxRowNZ) ; (C = minColNZ) ; (C = maxColNZ).

% Colour of a border cell (non‑zero)
border_colour(C) :- border(R,C), color(C).

% The colour at the top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle is the frame colour
frame_colour(F) :-
    border(R,C,F),
    R = minRowNZ,
    C = minColNZ.

% Global outer border of the whole grid (used for flood fill)
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Flood fill from the outer border through cells that are not the frame colour
global_outside(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
global_outside(R2,C2) :- global_outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior cells: non‑frame colour cells not reachable from the outer border
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not global_outside(R,C).

% Bounding box of interior region
int_minRow(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
int_maxRow(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
int_minCol(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_maxCol(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_minRow) ; (R = int_maxRow) ; (C = int_minCol) ; (C = int_maxCol).

% Output facts
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% No other colours for interior cells
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), corner(R,C), Col != 0.
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set – each interior cell appears exactly once
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior(R,C).
```

% --- Helper predicates for non‑zero cells ---
nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% --- Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells ---
minRowNZ(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
maxRowNZ(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C)}.
maxColNZ(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : nonzero(_,C)}.

% --- Border of that rectangle (only non‑zero colours matter for the frame) ---
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    R = minRowNZ, R = maxRowNZ, C = minColNZ, C = maxColNZ.

% --- The colour that appears at the top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    border(R,C,F),
    R = minRowNZ,
    C = minColNZ.

% --- Global outer border of the whole grid (used for flood fill) ---
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

% --- Offsets for 4‑connected movement ---
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% --- Flood fill from the outer grid border through cells that are NOT the frame colour ---
global_outside(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
global_outside(R2,C2) :- global_outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% --- Interior cells: non‑frame colour cells not reachable from the outer border ---
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not global_outside(R,C).

% --- Bounding box of the interior region ---
int_minRow(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_maxRow(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_minCol(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_maxCol(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% --- Corner of the interior rectangle (four extreme positions) ---
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = int_minRow) ; (R = int_maxRow) ; (C = int_minCol) ; (C = int_maxCol).

% --- Output facts for interior cells ---
%  Corner cells become 0 (if they were already 0) or become the frame colour (if they were non‑zero)
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
%  Non‑corner interior cells always become the frame colour
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% --- Prevent any other colour from being used for interior cells ---
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), corner(R,C), Col != 0.
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% --- Ensure exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once ---
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

<asp>
% --- Helper predicates for non‑zero cells ---
nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% --- Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells ---
minRowNZ(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
maxRowNZ(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C)}.
maxColNZ(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : nonzero(_,C)}.

% --- Border of that rectangle (only non‑zero colours matter for the frame) ---
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    R = minRowNZ, R = maxRowNZ, C = minColNZ, C = maxColNZ.

% --- The colour that appears at the top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    border(R,C,F),
    R = minRowNZ,
    C = minColNZ.

% --- Global outer border of the whole grid (used for flood fill) ---
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

% --- Offsets for 4‑connected movement ---
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% --- Flood fill from the outer grid border through cells that are NOT the frame colour ---
global_outside(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
global_outside(R2,C2) :- global_outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% --- Interior cells: non‑frame colour cells not reachable from the outer border ---
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not global_outside(R,C).

% --- Bounding box of the interior region ---
int_minRow(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_maxRow(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_minCol(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_maxCol(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% --- Corner of the interior rectangle (four extreme positions) ---
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = int_minRow) ; (R = int_maxRow) ; (C = int_minCol) ; (C = int_maxCol).

% --- Output facts for interior cells ---
%  Corner cells become 0 (if they were already 0) or become the frame colour (if they were non‑zero)
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
%  Non‑corner interior cells always become the frame colour
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% --- Prevent any other colour from being used for interior cells ---
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), corner(R,C), Col != 0.
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% --- Ensure exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once ---
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior(R,C).
</asp>

nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

minColNonZero(NC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_)} = NC.

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
minRowNonZero(NR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_)} = NR.
maxRowNonZero(NR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_)} = NR.
minColNonZero(NC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_)} = NC.
maxColNonZero(NC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_)} = NC.

% Frame colour: colour at top-left corner of bounding rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRowNonZero, minColNonZero, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Output dimension: interior rows = maxRowNonZero - minRowNonZero - 1, interior cols = maxColNonZero - minColNonZero - 1.
interior_row(IR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_)} = MaxR, #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_)} = MinR, IR = MaxR - MinR - 1.
interior_col(IC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_)} = MaxC, #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_)} = MinC, IC = MaxC - MinC - 1.

% We assume interior dimensions >0.

% For each interior cell (strictly inside bounding rectangle), output cell.
output(R0,C0,Col) :-
    interior(R,C), interior_col(IC), interior_row(IR), R0 = R - MinR - 1, C0 = C - MinC - 1,
    interior(R,C),
    (input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, Col = frame_colour) ;
    output(R0,C0,Col).

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero region
minRowNZ(NR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = NR.
maxRowNZ(NR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = NR.
minColNZ(NC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_)} = NC.
maxColNZ(NC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_)} = NC.

% Frame colour: colour at (minRowNZ, minColNZ) assuming it is non-zero
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRowNZ, minColNZ, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Global outside: cells reachable from outer border without crossing frame colour.
% Define outer border cells: any cell on the outer rectangle (row = minRowNZ or maxRowNZ or col = minColNZ or maxColNZ)
outer(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour,
    (R = minRowNZ) ; (R = maxRowNZ) ; (C = minColNZ) ; (C = maxColNZ).

% Flood fill from outer border through cells that are not frame_colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior: non-zero cells not on outer border and not reachable from outer border (i.e., interior region)
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour,

% Offsets

```asp
% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero region
minRow(NR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = NR.
maxRow(AR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = AR.
minCol(NC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = NC.
maxCol(MC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MC.

% Frame colour – colour of the cell at the top‑left corner of the rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Cells on the outer rectangle (the “frame”) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

% Flood fill from the outer rectangle through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Cells that belong to the interior region – non‑frame colour cells that are not reachable from the outer rectangle
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% Bounding box of the interior region
minRowI(IR) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = IR.
maxRowI(IR) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = IR.
minColI(JC) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = JC.
maxColI(JC) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = JC.

% Corner of the interior rectangle (four extreme positions)
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% Output facts
output(R_out, C_out, 0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R_out, C_out, F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R_out, C_out, F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% One answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
output_cell(R_out, C_out) :- interior(R,C), R_out = R - minRow - 1, C_out = C - minCol - 1.
1 { output(R_out, C_out, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R_out, C_out).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
```

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
minRow(MinR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% Frame colour – colour of the top‑left corner cell of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Frame border – cells on the outer rectangle that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

% Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior – non‑frame colour cells that cannot be reached from the outer rectangle
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding box of the interior region
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% Output facts
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one output per interior cell (scaled to a 0‑based output grid)
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell receives exactly one colour
1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
```

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
minRow(MinR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% Frame colour – colour at the top‑left corner of the rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Frame border – cells on the outer rectangle that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

% Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior – cells that are non‑frame colour and are NOT reachable from the outer border
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding box of the interior region
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% Output facts
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% One answer set: each interior cell must appear exactly once
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRow(MinR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells
%    that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Corner cells of the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Output facts
%    – corner interior cells become 0 (if they were 0) or become the frame colour
%    – all other interior cells become the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Offsets for 4‑connected movement
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRow(MinR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells
%    that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Corner cells of the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Output facts
%    – corner interior cells become 0 (if they were 0) or become the frame colour
%    – all other interior cells become the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

% --- Helper predicates for non‑zero cells ---
nonzero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% --- Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells ---
minRowNZ(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
maxRowNZ(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : nonzero(R,_) }.
minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C)}.
maxColNZ(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : nonzero(_,C)}.

% --- Border of that rectangle (only non‑zero colours matter for the frame) ---
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    R = minRowNZ, R = maxRowNZ, C = minColNZ, C = maxColNZ.

% --- The colour that appears at the top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    border(R,C,F),
    R = minRowNZ,
    C = minColNZ.

% --- Global outer border of the whole grid (used for flood fill) ---
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

% --- Offsets for 4‑connected movement ---
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% --- Flood fill from the outer grid border through cells that are NOT the frame colour ---
global_outside(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
global_outside(R2,C2) :- global_outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% --- Interior cells: non‑frame colour cells not reachable from the outer border ---
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not global_outside(R,C).

% --- Bounding box of the interior region ---
int_minRow(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_maxRow(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_minCol(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_maxCol(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% --- Corner of the interior rectangle (four extreme positions) ---
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = int_minRow) ; (R = int_maxRow) ; (C = int_minCol) ; (C = int_maxCol).

% --- Output facts for interior cells ---
%  Corner cells become 0 (if they were already 0) or become the frame colour (if they were non‑zero)
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
%  Non‑corner interior cells always become the frame colour
output(R,C,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% --- Prevent any other colour from being used for interior cells ---
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), corner(R,C), Col != 0.
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% --- Ensure exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once ---
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior(R,C).
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRow(MinR) :- #min{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{Row : nonzero(Row,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells
%    that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Corner cells of the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Output facts
%    – corner interior cells become 0 (if they were 0) or become the frame colour
%    – all other interior cells become the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Offsets for 4‑connected movement
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).
</asp>

We'll use the following approach:

% Define non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Compute bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
minRZ(NR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = NR.
maxRZ(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCZ(NC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = NC.
maxCZ(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

% Border cells: cells that lie on the outer border of the non-zero region
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minCZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxCZ.

% Count occurrences of each colour on border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% Determine frame colour as a colour with maximal border count (choose any if tie)
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border_colour(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F, count_border_colour(C2), count_border_colour(C2) > 0, count_border_colour(C2) > count_border_colour(F)).

% Define delta for 4-connectivity
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Define outer cells: cells on outer border of non-zero rectangle that are not the frame colour (including zeros)
outer_cell(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minRZ) ; (R = maxRZ) ; (C = minCZ) ; (C = maxCZ), V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood fill from outer cells through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer_cell(R,C,V).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minRZ, R2 <= maxRZ,
    C2 >= minCZ, C2 <= maxCZ.

% Interior non-zero cells: non-zero cells not on the frame colour and not reachable from the outer cells
interior_nz(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C).

% Determine bounding rectangle of interior non-zero cells
interior_minRow(MinR) :- #min{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = MinR.
interior_maxRow(MaxR) :- #max{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = MaxR.
interior_minCol(MinC) :- #min{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = MinC.
interior_maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = MaxC.

% Define rectangle of interior (including zeros inside that rectangle)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    R >= interior_minRow,
    R <= interior_maxRow,
    C >= interior_minCol,
    C <= interior_maxCol.

% Corners of interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    (R = interior_minRow) ; (R = interior_maxRow) ; (C = interior_minCol) ; (C = interior_maxCol).

% Output facts: interior rectangle cells become frame colour, corners become 0
output(R,C,F) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    not corner(R,C).

output(R,C,0) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    corner(R,C).

% Ensure each interior cell appears exactly once
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior_rect(R,C).

<asp>
% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
minRZ(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxRZ(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minCZ(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxCZ(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

% Border cells (any cell on outer side)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minCZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxCZ.

% Count border colours
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border_colour(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border_colour(C2), count_border_colour(C2) > 0,
        count_border_colour(C2) > count_border_colour(F)).

% Offsets for 4‑connectivity
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer cells: border cells that are NOT the frame colour (includes zeros)
outer_cell(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minRZ) ; (R = maxRZ) ; (C = minCZ) ; (C = maxCZ), V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood‑fill from outer cells through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer_cell(R,C,V).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minRZ, R2 <= maxRZ,
    C2 >= minCZ, C2 <= maxCZ.

% Interior non‑zero cells: non‑frame colour, not reachable from outer cells
interior_nz(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C).

% Bounds of interior non‑zero cells
interior_minRow(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = Imin.
interior_maxRow(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = Imax.
interior_minCol(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = Jmin.
interior_maxCol(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = Jmax.

% Rectangle covering the interior region (including zeros inside)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_nz(R,C,_),
    interior_minRow(IMinR), interior_maxRow(IMaxR), interior_minCol(IMinC), interior_maxCol(IMaxC),
    R >= IMinR, R <= IMaxR, C >= IMinC, C <= IMaxC.

% Corner predicate for the rectangle
corner(R,C) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    (R = IMinR), (R = IMaxR), (C = IMinC), (C = IMaxC).

% Output: interior rectangle cells become the frame colour,
% except the four corners become 0.
output(R,C,F)    :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

% Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).
</asp>

% Determine non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
minRZ(R0) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = R0.
maxRZ(R1) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = R1.
minCZ(C0) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = C0.
maxCZ(C1) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = C1.

% Border cells (any cell on outer side)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxRZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minCZ.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxCZ.

% Count border colour occurrences
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border_colour(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border_colour(C2), count_border_colour(C2) > 0,
        count_border_colour(C2) > count_border_colour(F)).

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer cells: cells on outer border that are NOT the frame colour (including zeros)
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minRZ) ; (R = maxRZ) ; (C = minCZ) ; (C = maxCZ), V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood fill from outer cells through cells that are not frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minRZ, R2 <= maxRZ,
    C2 >= minCZ, C2 <= maxCZ.

% Interior non‑frame colour cells (non‑zero and not reachable)
interior_nz(R,C,V,F) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C,F).

% Compute bounds of interior non‑frame colour cells
interior_minRow(IMinR) :- #min{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = IMinR.
interior_maxRow(IMaxR) :- #max{R : interior_nz(R,_) } = IMaxR.
interior_minCol(IMinC) :- #min{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = IMinC.
interior_maxCol(IMaxC) :- #max{C : interior_nz(_,C)} = IMaxC.

% Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    IMinR <= R, R <= IMaxR, IMinC <= C, C <= IMaxC.

% Corner predicate
corner(R,C) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    R = IMinR,
    R = IMaxR,
    C = IMinC,
    C = IMaxC.

% Output facts
output(R,C,F) :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C).
output(R,C,0) :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

% Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<asp>
% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

% Border cells (any cell on the outer side of the non‑zero region)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

% Count colour occurrences on the border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% Frame colour – the colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

% 4‑connected movement offsets
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Outer cells – cells on the outer side that are NOT the frame colour
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC), V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

% Interior non‑zero cells – non‑frame cells not reachable from the outside
interior_nz(R,C,V,F) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounding rectangle of interior non‑zero cells
int_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_nz(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
int_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_nz(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
int_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_nz(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
int_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_nz(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

% Rectangle covering the interior region (includes zeros inside the rectangle)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_nz(R,C,V,F),
    int_min_row(Imin), int_max_row(Imax), int_min_col(Jmin), int_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

% Corner predicate for the rectangle
corner(R,C) :-
    interior_rect(R,C),
    (R = Imin), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin), (C = Jmax).

% Output: interior rectangle cells become the frame colour, except the four corners which become 0.
output(R,C,frame_colour(F)) :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C).
output(R,C,0) :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

% Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

```
<asp>
% ---------- non‑zero cells ----------
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% ---------- bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells ----------
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

% ---------- border cells ----------
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

% ---------- count colour on border ----------
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% ---------- frame colour (most frequent colour on the border) ----------
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

% ---------- offsets ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ---------- outer cells (border cells that are NOT the frame colour) ----------
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC), V != frame_colour(F).

% ---------- flood fill ----------
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

% ---------- interior non‑zero cells ----------
interior_nz(R,C,V,F) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C,F).

% ---------- bounds of interior non‑zero cells ----------
int_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_nz(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
int_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_nz(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
int_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_nz(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
int_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_nz(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

% ---------- rectangle covering interior region (including zeros inside) ----------
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_nz(R,C,V,F),
    int_min_row(Imin), int_max_row(Imax), int_min_col(Jmin), int_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

% ---------- corner of the rectangle ----------
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

% ---------- output facts ----------
output(R,C,F) :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

% ---------- exactly one output per interior cell ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set (each interior cell gets exactly one colour)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>
