% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

% Border cells (any cell on outer side)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

% Count colour occurrences on border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

% Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

% Offsets for 4‑connectivity
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer cells: cells on the outer side that are NOT the frame colour
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC), V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood‑fill from outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

% Interior cells – any cell within the outer rectangle that is NOT reachable from the outside
interior_all(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), not outside(R,C,F), R >= minR, R <= maxR, C >= minC, C <= maxC.

% Bounds of interior region (including zeros)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_all(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_all(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

% Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros inside)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_all(R,C,V,F),
    interior_min_row(Imin), interior_max_row(Imax), interior_min_col(Jmin), interior_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

% Corner predicate for the rectangle

corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

% Output: interior rectangle cells become the frame colour, except the four corners which become 0
output(R,C,F) :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

% Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

```asp
% ==============================
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% ==============================
%  1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

%  3.  Border cells (any cell on the outer side of the non‑zero region)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

%  4.  Colour counts on the border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

%  5.  Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

%  5.1 offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

%  5.2 outer cells – border cells that are NOT the frame colour (zeros are allowed)
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V),
    (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC),
    V != frame_colour(F).

%  5.3 flood‑fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

%  6.  Cells that are inside the outer rectangle but NOT reachable from the border
interior_all(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), not outside(R,C,F),
    R >= minR, R <= maxR, C >= minC, C <= maxC.

%  7.  Bounds of the interior region (the interior rectangle)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_all(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_all(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

%  8.  Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros inside it)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_all(R,C,V,F),
    interior_min_row(Imin), interior_max_row(Imax),
    interior_min_col(Jmin), interior_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

%  9.  Corners of that rectangle (four corners become 0)
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

%  10.  Output facts
output(R,C,F)    :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

%  11.  Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

%  12.  Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------
frame_colour(F) :- input(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells
%    that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(MinRI) :- #min{R : interior(R,_) } = MinRI.
maxRowI(MaxRI) :- #max{R : interior(R,_) } = MaxRI.
minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Corner cells of the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Output facts
%    – corner interior cells become 0 (if they were 0) or become the frame colour
%    – all other interior cells become the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rout,Cout,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F)    :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(Rout,Cout) :-
    interior(R,C),
    Rout = R - minRow - 1,
    Cout = C - minCol - 1.

1 { output(Rout,Cout,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rout,Cout).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Offsets for 4‑connected movement
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% ==============================
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% ==============================
%  1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

%  3.  Border cells (any cell on the outer side of the non‑zero region)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

%  4.  Colour counts on the border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

%  5.  Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

%  5.1 offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

%  5.2 outer cells – border cells that are NOT the frame colour (zeros are allowed)
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V),
    (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC),
    V != frame_colour(F).

%  5.3 flood‑fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

%  6.  Cells that are inside the outer rectangle but NOT reachable from the border
interior_all(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), not outside(R,C,F),
    R >= minR, R <= maxR, C >= minC, C <= maxC.

%  7.  Bounds of the interior region (the interior rectangle)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_all(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_all(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

%  8.  Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros inside it)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_all(R,C,V,F),
    interior_min_row(Imin), interior_max_row(Imax),
    interior_min_col(Jmin), interior_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

%  9.  Corners of that rectangle (four corners become 0)
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

%  10.  Output facts
output(R,C,F)    :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

%  11.  Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

%  12.  Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
</asp>

% Determine non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero region
minRowNonZero(MinR) :- MinR = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_)}.
maxRowNonZero(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_)}.
minColNonZero(MinC) :- MinC = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
maxColNonZero(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour: colour at top-left corner (assuming all corners same)
frame_colour(F) :- nonzero(MinRowNonZero, MinColNonZero, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Global outer grid dimensions
maxRowGlobal(MaxRG) :- MaxRG = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(MaxCG) :- MaxCG = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not equal to frame colour
outer(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = MaxRG) ; (C = 0) ; (C = MaxCG),
            input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior cells: cells not reachable from outer border and not frame colour
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding rectangle of interior region
int_min_row(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_max_row(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_min_col(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_max_col(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corners of interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_min_row), (C = int_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_min_row), (C = int_max_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_max_row), (C = int_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_max_row), (C = int_max_col).

% Output for interior cells
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Ensure exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once and no other outputs
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_) } = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C). % no extra output

% Optional: ensure exactly one answer set (optional)
% 1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% End of program

% Input cells are given; color(0..9) facts given.

% Define non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero region
minRowNonZero(MinR) :- MinR = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
maxRowNonZero(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
minColNonZero(MinC) :- MinC = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
maxColNonZero(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour (colour at the top‑left corner of that rectangle)
frame_colour(F) :- nonzero(MinRowNonZero, MinColNonZero, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Global outer dimensions (the whole grid)
maxRowGlobal(MaxRG) :- MaxRG = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(MaxCG) :- MaxCG = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Cells on the outer edge of the whole grid that are not of the frame colour
outer(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = MaxRG) ; (C = 0) ; (C = MaxCG),
               input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the outer border
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding rectangle of the interior region
int_min_row(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_max_row(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_min_col(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_max_col(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_min_row), (C = int_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_min_row), (C = int_max_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_max_row), (C = int_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = int_max_row), (C = int_max_col).

% Output facts
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Ensure exactly one answer set:
% each interior cell gets exactly one output and no extra output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_) } = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C).

% No additional answer‑set choice needed

% Input facts and color(0..9) are injected automatically.

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Outer frame: colour at the top‑left corner of the bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
frame_colour(F) :- 
    (R = #min{R2 : nonzero(R2,_,_)}) ; 
    (C = #min{C2 : nonzero(_,C2,_)}) ; 
    nonzero(R,C,F) ; 
    color(F) ; 
    F != 0.

% Global outer grid dimensions
maxRowGlobal(MaxRG) :- MaxRG = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(MaxCG) :- MaxCG = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement

% Outer border cells (on the edge of the whole grid) that are not the frame colour

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).

% Interior cells – non‑frame cells that are not reachable from the outer border

corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #min{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) ; (C = #max{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #max{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) ; (C = #min{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).

output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once, no extra output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_) } = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C).

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.

% Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

```
% Input cells and colors are given automatically

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
minRowNZ(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
maxRowNZ(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
maxColNZ(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at the top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- nonzero(minRowNZ, minColNZ, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Global outer grid dimensions
maxRowGlobal(Row) :- Row = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(Col) :- Col = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer border cells (grid edge) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ;
    (R = maxRowGlobal) ;
    (C = 0) ;
    (C = maxColGlobal) ;
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not equal to the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior cells – non‑frame cells not reachable from the outer border
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding rectangle of the interior region
minRowI(R2) :- R2 = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
maxRowI(R3) :- R3 = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
minColI(C2) :- C2 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C3) :- C3 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = minRowI), (C = minColI).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = minRowI), (C = maxColI).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = maxRowI), (C = minColI).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = maxRowI), (C = maxColI).

% Output facts
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once, no other output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_) } = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C).

```

```
% Input and color facts are given automatically

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the non‑zero region
minRow = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
maxRow = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
minCol = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
maxCol = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at the top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :- nonzero(minRow, minCol, F), color(F), F != 0.

% Global outer dimensions (for flood fill)
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Cells on the outer edge of the whole grid that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ;
    (R = maxRowGlobal) ;
    (C = 0) ;
    (C = maxColGlobal) ;
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Flood‑fill from the outer edge through cells that are not the frame colour
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% Interior cells – non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the outer edge
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% Bounding rectangle of the interior region
interior_min_row(I) :- I = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
interior_max_row(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
interior_min_col(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
interior_max_col(J) :- J = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = interior_min_row), (C = interior_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = interior_min_row), (C = interior_max_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = interior_max_row), (C = interior_min_col).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = interior_max_row), (C = interior_max_col).

% Output facts
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once, no extra output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_) } = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C).

```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

delta(0,-1).

    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% --- Bounding box of the interior region ---

:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------

nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells
%    that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------

% 6.  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------

minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Output facts
%    – corner interior cells become 0 (if they were 0) or become the frame colour

% ==============================
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% ==============================
%  1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

%  3.  Border cells (any cell on the outer side of the non‑zero region)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

%  4.  Colour counts on the border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

%  5.  Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

%  5.1 offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

%  5.2 outer cells – border cells that are NOT the frame colour (zeros are allowed)
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V),
    (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC),
    V != frame_colour(F).

%  5.3 flood‑fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

%  6.  Cells that are inside the outer rectangle but NOT reachable from the border
interior_all(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), not outside(R,C,F),
    R >= minR, R <= maxR, C >= minC, C <= maxC.

%  7.  Bounds of the interior region (the interior rectangle)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_all(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_all(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

%  8.  Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros inside it)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_all(R,C,V,F),
    interior_min_row(Imin), interior_max_row(Imax),
    interior_min_col(Jmin), interior_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

%  9.  Corners of that rectangle (four corners become 0)
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

%  10.  Output facts
output(R,C,F)    :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

%  11.  Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

%  12.  Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<asp>
% Input and color facts are injected automatically

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates for non‑zero cells
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% The frame colour is the colour that appears at the top‑left corner
% of the bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
frame_colour(F) :-
    (R = #min{R2 : nonzero(R2,_,_)}) ;
    (C = #min{C2 : nonzero(_,C2,_)}) ;
    nonzero(R,C,F) ;
    color(F) ;
    F != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells on the outer edge of the whole grid that are not the frame colour
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
outer(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ;
    (R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}) ;
    (C = 0) ;
    (C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)}) ;
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are not the frame colour
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Interior cells – non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the outer border
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not outside(R,C).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells of the interior rectangle (four extreme positions)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #min{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) , (C = #min{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #min{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) , (C = #max{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #max{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) , (C = #min{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = #max{R2 : interior(R2,_)}) , (C = #max{C2 : interior(_,C2)}).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output facts
%   – corners become 0 if they were already 0, otherwise they become the frame colour
%   – all other interior cells become the frame colour
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once, no extra output
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_)} = #count{R : interior(R,_) }.
:- output(R,C,_), not interior(R,C).
</asp>

Implementation:

% Determine global dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
nz_min_row(RowMin) :- RowMin = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_max_row(RowMax) :- RowMax = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_min_col(ColMin) :- ColMin = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
nz_max_col(ColMax) :- ColMax = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour at top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nz_min_row(R1), nz_min_col(C1),
    input(R1, C1, F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    nonzero(R1, C1, F).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Global outer border cells (not frame colour)
outer_cell(R,C,F) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    (R = 0) ; (R = max_row) ; (C = 0) ; (C = max_col),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= max_row, C2 >= 0, C2 <= max_col,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells: non‑frame coloured cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounding rectangle of interior cells
int_min_row(Imin) :- Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_max_row(Imax) :- Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_min_col(Jmin) :- Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_max_col(Jmax) :- Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Cells inside the interior rectangle (including zeros)
rect(R,C) :-
    int_min_row(Imin), int_max_row(Imax), int_min_col(Jmin), int_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :-
    rect(R,C),
    (R = Imin) ; (R = Imax) ; (C = Jmin) ; (C = Jmax).

% Output facts
output(Rout,Colout,0) :-
    rect(R,C),
    corner(R,C),
    R - Imin = Rout,
    C - Jmin = Colout,
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V = 0.

output(Rout,Colout,F) :-
    rect(R,C),
    corner(R,C),
    R - Imin = Rout,
    C - Jmin = Colout,
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    frame_colour(F).

output(Rout,Colout,F) :-
    rect(R,C),
    not corner(R,C),
    R - Imin = Rout,
    C - Jmin = Colout,
    frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set: each interior rectangle cell appears exactly once
output_cell(R,C) :- rect(R,C).

:- #count{F : frame_colour(F)} = 1.
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_)} = #count{R : output_cell(R,C)}.
:- not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell(R,C).

% Global dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
nz_min_row(Rnz) :- Rnz = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_max_row(Rnz) :- Rnz = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_min_col(Cnz) :- Cnz = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
nz_max_col(Cnz) :- Cnz = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour at top-left of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nz_min_row(Rnz), nz_min_col(Cnz),
    input(Rnz, Cnz, F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    nonzero(Rnz, Cnz, F).

% Offsets for 4-connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer border cells (global border) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    (R = 0) ; (R = max_row) ; (C = 0) ; (C = max_col),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= max_row,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= max_col,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells: non-frame coloured cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Interior bounds
int_min_row(Imin) :- Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_max_row(Imax) :- Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_min_col(Jmin) :- Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_max_col(Jmax) :- Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Cells inside the interior rectangle (including zeros)
rect(Rabs,Cabs) :-
    int_min_row(Imin), int_max_row(Imax), int_min_col(Jmin), int_max_col(Jmax),
    Rabs >= Imin, Rabs <= Imax,
    Cabs >= Jmin, Cabs <= Jmax.

% Allowed colour for a cell (absolute coordinates)
% 1. Corner cell with original colour 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V = 0, Col = 0.
% 2. Non‑corner cells become frame colour
allowed(R,C,F) :- not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F), Col = F.
% 3. Corner cell with original colour != 0 becomes frame colour
allowed(R,C,F) :- corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F), Col = F.

% Need corner predicate
corner(R,C) :- rect(R,C), (R = Imin) ; (R = Imax) ; (C = Jmin) ; (C = Jmax).

% Output cell definition: absolute coordinates mapped to relative and colour allowed
output_cell(Rout,Colout) :-
    rect(Rabs,Cabs),
    allowed(Rabs,Cabs,Col),
    Rout = Rabs - Imin,
    Colout = Cabs - Jmin.

% Exactly one answer set: each output_cell gets exactly one output fact
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Prevent extra outputs: a cell must be output only if it is in the interior rectangle
:- not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell(R,C).

% Ensure a unique frame colour
:- #count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

```
% Global dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
nz_min_row(Rnz) :- Rnz = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_max_row(Rnz) :- Rnz = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_min_col(Cnz) :- Cnz = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
nz_max_col(Cnz) :- Cnz = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nz_min_row(Rnz), nz_min_col(Cnz),
    input(Rnz, Cnz, F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    nonzero(Rnz, Cnz, F).

% Outer border cells (global border) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    (R = 0) ; (R = max_row) ; (C = 0) ; (C = max_col),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= max_row,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= max_col,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells: non‑frame coloured cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Interior bounds
int_min_row(Imin) :- Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_max_row(Imax) :- Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_min_col(Jmin) :- Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
int_max_col(Jmax) :- Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Cells inside the interior rectangle (including zeros)
rect(Rabs,Cabs) :-
    int_min_row(Imin), int_max_row(Imax), int_min_col(Jmin), int_max_col(Jmax),
    Rabs >= Imin, Rabs <= Imax,
    Cabs >= Jmin, Cabs <= Jmax.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle
corner(Rabs,Cabs) :-
    rect(Rabs,Cabs),
    Rabs = Imin, Rabs = Imax,
    Cabs = Jmin, Cabs = Jmax.

% Allowed colour for a cell (absolute coordinates)
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,0) :- corner(Rabs,Cabs), input(Rabs,Cabs,V), color(V), V = 0.
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,F) :- not corner(Rabs,Cabs), frame_colour(F).
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,F) :- corner(Rabs,Cabs), input(Rabs,Cabs,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).

% Absolute‑coordinate output cell (will be used by the block)
output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs) :- rect(Rabs,Cabs).

% Block that creates exactly one output fact for each interior‑rectangle cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs), allowed(Rabs,Cabs,Col), 
   R = Rabs - Imin, 
   C = Cabs - Jmin.

% Ensure a unique frame colour
:- #count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

% Prevent any extra output (any output must correspond to an interior‑rectangle cell)
:- not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs).

% End of program
```

% Helper predicates for non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- MinR = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
maxR(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
minC(MinC) :- MinC = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
maxC(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Border of that rectangle (only non‑zero colours matter for the frame) 
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

% The colour that appears at the top‑left corner of the non‑zero rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    border(R,C,F),
    R = minR,
    C = minC.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Cells on the outer grid border that are not the frame colour (zeros are allowed)
outer(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V),
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxC),
    V != frame_colour(F).

% Flood‑fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F).

% Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells that are NOT reachable from the outer border
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour(F),
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounding box of the interior region (the interior rectangle)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = Imin) ; (R = Imax) ; (C = Jmin) ; (C = Jmax).

% Output facts
output(Rout,Cout,0) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(Rout,Cout,F) :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).
output(Rout,Cout,F) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).

% Prevent any other colour from being used for interior cells
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), corner(R,C), Col != 0.
:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once, no extra output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
:- #count{R : output(R,_,_)} = #count{R : output_cell(R,C)}.
:- output(R,C,_) , not interior(R,C).

```
% Global dimensions for outer border (flood fill)
maxRow = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
MinRowNZ = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MaxRowNZ = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MinColNZ = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
MaxColNZ = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at the top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nonzero(R,C,F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    R = MinRowNZ,
    C = MinColNZ.

% Outer border cells (global border) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRow) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxCol),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= maxRow,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= maxCol,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounds of interior region
Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Cells inside the interior rectangle (including zeros)
rect(Rabs,Cabs) :- Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }, Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) },
    Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}, Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)},
    Rabs >= Imin, Rabs <= Imax,
    Cabs >= Jmin, Cabs <= Jmax.

% Corner cells of the interior rectangle (any cell on its border)
corner(Rabs,Cabs) :- interior(Rabs,Cabs),
    (Rabs = Imin) ; (Rabs = Imax) ; (Cabs = Jmin) ; (Cabs = Jmax).

% Allowed colour for a cell
% 1. Corner cell that was originally zero
allowed(R,C,0) :- corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V = 0.

% 2. Non‑corner interior cell becomes the frame colour
allowed(R,C,F) :- not corner(R,C), interior(R,C), frame_colour(F), R = Imin, C = Jmin.

% 3. Corner cell with original non‑zero becomes the frame colour
allowed(R,C,F) :- corner(R,C), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).

% Absolute‑coordinate output cell (used by the cardinality rule)
output_cell(Rabs,Cabs) :- rect(Rabs,Cabs).

% Output facts for each interior‑rectangle cell
output(R,C,Col) :-
    output_cell(Rabs,Cabs),
    allowed(Rabs,Cabs,Col),
    R = Rabs - Imin,
    C = Cabs - Jmin.

% Exactly one answer set – each interior‑rectangle cell appears exactly once
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rabs,Cabs).

% Ensure a unique frame colour
:- #count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

% No extra output outside the interior rectangle
:- not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell(Rabs,Cabs).
```

We'll define:

% Bounding rectangle of non-zero cells
nz_min_row = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_max_row = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
nz_min_col = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
nz_max_col = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

frame_colour(F) :- nonzero(R,C,F), color(F), F != 0, R = nz_min_row, C = nz_min_col.

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outer border cells (global border)
outer(R,C,F) :- (R = 0) ; (R = GridMaxRow) ; (C = 0) ; (C = GridMaxCol), input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :- outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells
interior(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour(F), not outside(R,C,F).

% Interior bounds
Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Cells inside interior rectangle
rect(Rabs,Cabs) :- Rabs >= Imin, Rabs <= Imax, Cabs >= Jmin, Cabs <= Jmax.

% Corner cells (any cell on border of interior rectangle)
corner(Rabs,Cabs) :- interior(Rabs,Cabs), (Rabs = Imin) ; (Rabs = Imax) ; (Cabs = Jmin) ; (Cabs = Jmax).

% Allowed colour
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,0) :- corner(Rabs,Cabs), input(Rabs,Cabs,V), color(V), V = 0.
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,F) :- not corner(Rabs,Cabs), interior(Rabs,Cabs), frame_colour(F), Rabs = Imin, Cabs = Jmin.
allowed(Rabs,Cabs,F) :- corner(Rabs,Cabs), input(Rabs,Cabs,V), color(V), V != 0, frame_colour(F).

% Absolute‑coordinate output cell (used by cardinality rule)
output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs) :- rect(Rabs,Cabs).

% Output for each cell (relative coordinates)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs),
    allowed(Rabs,Cabs,Col),
    R = Rabs - Imin,
    C = Cabs - Jmin.

% Exactly one answer set: each interior‑rectangle cell appears exactly once
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs).

% Unique frame colour
#count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

% No extra output
not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell_abs(Rabs,Cabs).

```
% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Offsets for outer border (grid dimensions)
GridMaxRow = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
GridMaxCol = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
MinRowNZ = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MaxRowNZ = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MinColNZ = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
MaxColNZ = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nonzero(R,C,F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    R = MinRowNZ,
    C = MinColNZ.

% Flood‑fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :- (R = 0) ; (R = GridMaxRow) ; (C = 0) ; (C = GridMaxCol),
                input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= GridMaxRow,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= GridMaxCol,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells – non‑frame colour cells not reachable
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Interior rectangle bounds
IntMinRow = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
IntMaxRow = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
IntMinCol = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
IntMaxCol = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner predicate (any cell on the border of the interior rectangle)
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = IntMinRow) ; (R = IntMaxRow) ; (C = IntMinCol) ; (C = IntMaxCol).

% Output for interior cells
output(Rout,Cout,0) :-
    interior(R,C),
    corner(R,C),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V = 0,
    Rout = R - IntMinRow,
    Cout = C - IntMinCol.
output(Rout,Cout,F) :-
    interior(R,C),
    corner(R,C),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    frame_colour(F),
    Rout = R - IntMinRow,
    Cout = C - IntMinCol.
output(Rout,Cout,F) :-
    interior(R,C),
    not corner(R,C),
    frame_colour(F),
    Rout = R - IntMinRow,
    Cout = C - IntMinCol.

% Exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Ensure a unique frame colour
:- #count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

% No extra output for non‑interior cells
:- not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell(R,C).

```
```
% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Global grid size (max indices)
GridMaxRow = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
GridMaxCol = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
MinRow = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MaxRow = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MinCol = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
MaxCol = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nonzero(R,C,F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    R = MinRow,
    C = MinCol.

% Outer border cells (global border) that are not the frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :- (R = 0) ; (R = GridMaxRow) ; (C = 0) ; (C = GridMaxCol),
                input(R,C,V), color(V), V != F.

% Flood fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= GridMaxRow,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= GridMaxCol,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells – non‑frame coloured cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounds of the interior region
Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
```

```
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Global dimensions (max index)
GridMaxRow = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
GridMaxCol = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of non‑zero cells
MinRow = #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MaxRow = #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) }.
MinCol = #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.
MaxCol = #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) }.

% Frame colour – colour at the top‑left corner of that rectangle
frame_colour(F) :-
    nonzero(R,C,F),
    color(F),
    F != 0,
    R = MinRow,
    C = MinCol.

% Flood‑fill from outer border through cells not of frame colour
outer(R,C,F) :- (R = 0) ; (R = GridMaxRow) ; (C = 0) ; (C = GridMaxCol),

outside(R,C,F) :- outer(R,C,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    R2 >= 0, R2 <= GridMaxRow,
    C2 >= 0, C2 <= GridMaxCol,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != F.

% Interior cells – non‑frame coloured cells not reachable from outer border
interior(R,C) :-
    frame_colour(F),
    nonzero(R,C,V), V != F,
    not outside(R,C,F).

% Bounds of the interior region
Imin = #min{R : interior(R,_) }.
Imax = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
Jmin = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
Jmax = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% Corner cells (any cell on the border of the interior rectangle)
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C), (R = Imin) ; (R = Imax) ; (C = Jmin) ; (C = Jmax).

% Output for each interior cell

    interior(R,C),
    corner(R,C),

    Rout = R - Imin,
    Cout = C - Jmin.

    frame_colour(F),
    Rout = R - Imin,
    Cout = C - Jmin.

% Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Unique frame colour
:- #count(F : frame_colour(F)) = 1.

% No extra output outside the interior rectangle
not output(R,C,_) : not output_cell(R,C).
```

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI puzzle: “Frame‑fill” transformation
% -------------------------------------------------
% The rule is:
%   • Find the colour C that appears on the outer boundary of the
%     non‑zero region (the colour with the greatest count on the
%     rows/cols that are min/max for non‑zero cells).
%   • Flood‑fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are
%     NOT of colour C.  Cells that are NOT reached become the interior.
%   • Every interior cell gets colour C, except the four corners of the
%     interior rectangle – those become 0.
%   • All other cells are 0 (they are not output at all).

% Offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Global dimensions of the whole grid
% -------------------------------------------------
maxRowGlobal(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
minRowGlobal(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.
minColGlobal(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Border of the non‑zero region (the “frame”)
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowNonZero(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxRowNonZero(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,V), color(V), V != 0}.
minColNonZero(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.
maxColNonZero(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,V), color(V), V != 0}.

% Cells that lie on that outer border
border_boundary(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0,
    (R = minRowNonZero) ; (R = maxRowNonZero) ; (C = minColNonZero) ; (C = maxColNonZero).

% Count how many of each colour are on that outer border
count_border_colour(C) :- #count{R : border_boundary(R,C)}.

% The frame colour – the most frequent colour on the outer border
outer_colour(C) :-
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    count_border_colour(C),
    count_border_colour(C2),
    C != C2,
    count_border_colour(C) > count_border_colour(C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood fill from the outer grid boundary through cells that are NOT the frame colour
% -------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != outer_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – those non‑frame cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), color(V), V != outer_colour,
    not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Bounding box of the interior region
% -------------------------------------------------
minRowI(R0) :- R0 = #min{R : interior(R,_)}.
maxRowI(R1) :- R1 = #max{R : interior(R,_)}.
minColI(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.
maxColI(C1) :- C1 = #max{C : interior(_,C)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Corner predicate for the interior rectangle
% -------------------------------------------------
corner(R,C) :- interior(R,C),
    (R = minRowI) ; (R = maxRowI) ; (C = minColI) ; (C = maxColI).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output facts
% -------------------------------------------------
% Corner cells become 0, all other interior cells become the frame colour
output(R,C,0)    :- interior(R,C), corner(R,C).
output(R,C,outer_colour) :- interior(R,C), not corner(R,C).

% Every interior cell must appear exactly once in the output
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one answer set – each interior cell gets exactly one colour
% -------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

minColNZ(C0) :- C0 = #min{C : nonzero(_,C)}.

maxColGlobal(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2)}.

% --- Offsets for 4‑connected movement ---
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

global_outside(R,C) :- (R = 0) ; (R = maxRowGlobal) ; (C = 0) ; (C = maxColGlobal),

global_outside(R2,C2) :- global_outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% --- Interior cells: non‑frame colour cells not reachable from the outer border ---
interior(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != frame_colour, not global_outside(R,C).

% --- Bounding box of the interior region ---

int_maxRow(I) :- I = #max{R : interior(R,_) }.
int_minCol(J) :- J = #min{C : interior(_,C)}.

:- interior(R,C), output(R,C,Col), not corner(R,C), Col != frame_colour(F).

% --- Ensure exactly one answer set: each interior cell appears exactly once ---
output_cell(R,C) :- interior(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), interior(R,C).

nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

minCol(MinC) :- #min{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MinC.
maxCol(MaxC) :- #max{Col : nonzero(_,Col,_) } = MaxC.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Frame colour – the colour at the top‑left corner
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Flood‑fill from the outer rectangle through cells

outer(R,C) :- nonzero(R,C,V), V != frame_colour,
    R = minRow ; R = maxRow ; C = minCol ; C = maxCol.

outside(R,C) :- outer(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    nonzero(R2,C2,V), color(V), V != frame_colour.

% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------

minColI(MinCI) :- #min{C : interior(_,C)} = MinCI.
maxColI(MaxCI) :- #max{C : interior(_,C)} = MaxCI.

% -------------------------------------------------

% ==============================
%  Frame‑fill transformation
% ==============================
%  1.  Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), color(V), V != 0.

%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
minR(MinR) :- #min{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MinR.
maxR(MaxR) :- #max{R : nonzero(R,_,_) } = MaxR.
minC(MinC) :- #min{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MinC.
maxC(MaxC) :- #max{C : nonzero(_,C,_) } = MaxC.

%  3.  Border cells (any cell on the outer side of the non‑zero region)
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = minR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), R = maxR.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = minC.
border(R,C,V) :- nonzero(R,C,V), C = maxC.

%  4.  Colour counts on the border
count_border(C) :- #count{R : border(R,C)}.

%  5.  Frame colour – colour with maximal border count
frame_colour(F) :-
    count_border(F),
    not (color(C2), C2 != F,
        count_border(C2), count_border(C2) > 0,
        count_border(C2) > count_border(F)).

%  5.1 offsets for 4‑connected movement
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

%  5.2 outer cells – border cells that are NOT the frame colour (zeros are allowed)
outer_cell(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), color(V),
    (R = minR) ; (R = maxR) ; (C = minC) ; (C = maxC),
    V != frame_colour(F).

%  5.3 flood‑fill from the outer cells through cells that are NOT the frame colour
outside(R,C,F) :- outer_cell(R,C,V,F).
outside(R2,C2,F) :-
    outside(R1,C1,F), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), color(V2), V2 != frame_colour(F),
    R2 >= minR, R2 <= maxR,
    C2 >= minC, C2 <= maxC.

%  6.  Cells that are inside the outer rectangle but NOT reachable from the border
interior_all(R,C,V,F) :- input(R,C,V), not outside(R,C,F),
    R >= minR, R <= maxR, C >= minC, C <= maxC.

%  7.  Bounds of the interior region (the interior rectangle)
interior_min_row(Imin) :- #min{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imin.
interior_max_row(Imax) :- #max{R : interior_all(R,_,_,_) } = Imax.
interior_min_col(Jmin) :- #min{C : interior_all(_,C,_,_) } = Jmin.
interior_max_col(Jmax) :- #max{C : interior_all(_,_,C,_) } = Jmax.

%  8.  Rectangle that covers the interior region (including zeros inside it)
interior_rect(R,C) :-
    interior_all(R,C,V,F),
    interior_min_row(Imin), interior_max_row(Imax),
    interior_min_col(Jmin), interior_max_col(Jmax),
    R >= Imin, R <= Imax, C >= Jmin, C <= Jmax.

%  9.  Corners of that rectangle (four corners become 0)
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmin).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imin), (C = Jmax).
corner(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C), (R = Imax), (C = Jmax).

%  10.  Output facts
output(R,C,F)    :- interior_rect(R,C), not corner(R,C), frame_colour(F).
output(R,C,0)    :- interior_rect(R,C), corner(R,C).

%  11.  Exactly one output per interior cell
output_cell(R,C) :- interior_rect(R,C).

%  12.  Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
